case_name: Sanjay Chandra vs Central Bureau of Investigation
summary: Landmark judgment establishing that detention beyond half the maximum sentence is unconstitutional
key_holding: Section 436A CrPC provides an absolute right to bail when an accused has been in detention for a period equal to half of the maximum sentence prescribed for the offence of which he is accused.
impact: Revolutionary - made mandatory bail a constitutional right
full_text: This Hon'ble Court held that an accused cannot be detained in custody beyond half the period of maximum sentence. This is a constitutional safeguard against illegal detention and arbitrary use of detention.
